network.overall_strategy: The synthesis network constructs L-Proline through convergent pathways from simple precursors, integrating hydrothermal reduction of CO2 to pyruvate and oxaloacetate via proto-metabolic cycles like HKG/malonate, surface Strecker and reductive routes to glycine, and biochemical assembly via formaldehyde addition to glycine or cyclization from glutamate. This addresses literature gaps in direct cyclic amino acid synthesis by providing redundant environmental interconnections (hydrothermal effluents to surface pools) and speculative extensions grounded in known intermediates (glyoxylate, oxaloacetate), with enantioselectivity in final steps. Mineral catalysts are specified per environment, and reactions cite supporting studies.

reactions.name: CO₂ hydrogenation to pyruvate
reactions.mechanism: Fischer-Tropsch-type surface hydrogenation of CO₂ to C3 keto acid
reactions.reasoning: Pyruvate as key prebiotic intermediate from CO₂ reduction in hydrothermal systems with Fe²⁺ catalysis at ~70°C (sources 9400, 9983); aligns with iron-sulfur world chemistry (Huber & Wächtershäuser, 1997)

reactions.name: Strecker synthesis of glycine
reactions.mechanism: Imine formation from aldehyde and ammonia, cyanide addition to aminonitrile, hydrolysis to amino acid
reactions.reasoning: Classic Strecker pathway for α-amino acids including glycine under prebiotic surface conditions (sources 6940, 6942; Miller, 1953); montmorillonite concentrates reactants (sources 503)

reactions.name: Reductive condensation to oxaloacetate

conditions.other: wet-dry cycles; inputs from hydrothermal pyruvate

reactions.mechanism: Aldol-type condensation of pyruvate and glyoxylate, followed by reduction
reactions.reasoning: Glyoxylate-dependent HKG cycle produces oxaloacetate from pyruvate in prebiotic surface environments (sources 10738); addresses protometabolic interconnections (Morowitz et al., 2000)

reactions.name: Reductive amination of glyoxylate to glycine
reactions.mechanism: Imine formation and surface-mediated reduction to amino acid
reactions.reasoning: Reductive amination on mineral surfaces in hydrothermal settings yields amino acids like glycine (sources 9396, 9334); provides alternative to Strecker (Huber & Wächtershäuser, 1997)

reactions.name: Formaldehyde addition to glycine forming L-proline
reactions.mechanism: Sequential Mannich-type additions of formaldehyde to glycine, forming imino chain that cyclizes with dehydration
reactions.reasoning: Experimental prebiotic synthesis via formaldehyde addition to glycine yields proline, sarcosine, and betaine (sources 2595); enantioselectivity via amplification (Levine et al., 2008); montmorillonite aids concentration though not direct agent

reactions.name: Reductive carboxylation to α-ketoglutarate
reactions.mechanism: Carboxylation of oxaloacetate analog with reduction on mineral surface
reactions.reasoning: Extension of malonate/HKG cycles to C5 keto acids in hydrothermal protometabolism (speculative but necessary to connect to glutamate; sources 10738, 9983; Eschenmoser, 1999)

reactions.name: Reductive amination to glutamic acid
reactions.mechanism: Imine formation from keto acid and ammonia, reduction to amino acid
reactions.reasoning: Prebiotic reductive amination of α-ketoglutarate to glutamate on clay surfaces (sources 9334, 9396); connects hydrothermal keto acids to biochemical assembly (Blackmond et al., year from sources 21298)

reactions.name: Cyclization of glutamic acid to L-proline
reactions.mechanism: Thermal decarboxylation to glutamic semialdehyde, spontaneous imine cyclization and reduction
reactions.reasoning: Speculative prebiotic cyclization analogous to biological pathway, using heat in geothermal cycling to form cyclic amino acids; addresses literature challenge of cyclic structure formation from linear precursors (sources 6808, 21194); supported by dry-wet cycles for condensation (sources 512)

pathways.name: Surface Strecker pathway via glycine
pathways.description: Direct surface synthesis of glycine followed by biochemical addition and cyclization

pathways.name: Hydrothermal reductive amination pathway via glycine
pathways.description: Hydrothermal formation of glycine from glyoxylate, then surface/biochemical to proline; demonstrates hydrothermal-surface flow

pathways.name: Proto-metabolic HKG pathway via glutamate
pathways.description: Hydrothermal reduction to pyruvate, surface aldol to oxaloacetate, extension to α-ketoglutarate, amination to glutamate, cyclization; interconnects environments via keto acid hubs

pathways.name: Alternative surface aldol integration pathway
pathways.description: Hydrothermal pyruvate feeds into surface condensation with glyoxylate, then hydrothermal extension to glutamate route

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-dominant HKG extension
pathways.description: Full hydrothermal sequence from pyruvate to oxaloacetate to α-ketoglutarate, then biochemical amination and cyclization

pathways.name: Glyoxylate-centric reductive pathway
pathways.description: Hydrothermal amination of glyoxylate to glycine, integrated with surface wet-dry for proline formation

pathways.name: Convergent keto acid to amino acid cyclization
pathways.description: From surface oxaloacetate (fed by hydrothermal pyruvate) directly to glutamate via extended cycle

pathways.name: Strecker with hydrothermal H₂ input
pathways.description: Surface Strecker using H₂ from hydrothermal origins for implicit reduction steps

pathways.name: Integrated tidal flat wet-dry pathway
pathways.description: Surface glycine synthesis in tidal flats, followed by dry-wet cyclization to proline

pathways.name: Full network convergence via multiple hubs
pathways.description: Hydrothermal pyruvate to multiple branches: glycine or glutamate routes to proline

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Surface Strecker to glycine followed by formaldehyde addition aligns well with literature on Strecker and glycine-proline formation.
suggestions: Specify mineral catalysts for surface Strecker.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Hydrothermal reductive amination to glycine then to proline; good flow from hydrothermal to biochemical.
suggestions: Add details on H2 source in hydrothermal setting.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: HKG pathway via glutamate cyclization; strong literature alignment with oxaloacetate and HKG cycles, addresses cyclic synthesis gap.
suggestions: Justify reductive condensation in rxn_003 with mineral catalysis; explore prebiotic cyclization mechanisms.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Alternative surface aldol integration; plausible but less direct literature support for aldol in proline path.
suggestions: Incorporate Mannich-like reaction for better alignment.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Hydrothermal-dominant HKG; good environmental focus, aligns with low-temp hydrothermal vents.
suggestions: Specify Fe-catalyzed conditions for hydrothermal steps.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Glyoxylate-centric reductive; strong on intermediates like glyoxylate from literature.
suggestions: Link to tidal flat environments for glyoxylate stability.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Convergent keto acid to amino cyclization; coherent but speculative for prebiotic cyclization.
suggestions: Add dry-wet cycle for cyclization step.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Strecker with hydrothermal H2; integrates environments well.
suggestions: Clarify H2 transfer from hydrothermal to surface.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Integrated tidal flat wet-dry; aligns with literature on tidal pools and cycles.
Full network convergence via hubs; demonstrates redundancy and interconnection.

evaluation.network_feedback: Well-connected network with convergent pathways aligning with literature on Strecker, HKG cycles, and formaldehyde addition; addresses cyclic synthesis challenges through redundant routes, though some steps like cyclization are speculative and lack direct prebiotic evidence.
